metadata.title: Jurisdiction Divergence Verification Report
metadata.methodology: Each divergence claim verified against source PDFs in stage-1/ directories (Title 21 CFR, EU Directives/Regulations, EU GMP Annexes, PMDA guidelines, JP18, Health Canada FDR, WHO TRS, ICH guidelines). Claims checked for (1) accuracy of cited legal basis, (2) accuracy of regulatory position described, (3) accuracy of the divergence characterization.

summary.overall_accuracy_rate: 83.4% fully confirmed, 11.3% need minor clarification, 2.0% false, 3.3% unverifiable from available sources

critical_findings.finding: BCS BIOWAIVER CONTRADICTION — Two files state opposite wrong positions
critical_findings.details: v2 DIV-004 claims 'FDA: Class I only; EMA: Class I AND Class III'. eu-gmp DIV-016 claims the OPPOSITE: 'EU BCS biowaiver only for Class I; FDA BCS biowaiver for Class I AND Class III'. BOTH ARE WRONG. Post-ICH M9 (adopted Nov 2019, FDA implementation May 2021), ALL three jurisdictions (FDA, EMA, PMDA) accept BCS Class I AND Class III biowaivers. The remaining divergence is only in the stringency of excipient similarity requirements and dissolution speed criteria for Class III.
critical_findings.source_evidence: ICH M9 Step 4 guideline (Nov 2019): 'BCS-based biowaivers are applicable to drug products where the drug substance(s) exhibit high solubility and, either high permeability (BCS Class I) or low permeability (BCS Class III).' Found at stage-1/ich-complete/M9_Guideline_Step4_2019_1116.pdf
critical_findings.action_required: MUST FIX both files immediately. Rewrite to reflect post-ICH M9 harmonized position.

critical_findings.finding: RSABE SCOPE ERROR — eu-gmp file overstates FDA RSABE applicability
critical_findings.details: DIV-016 claims 'FDA RSABE allowed for BOTH AUC and Cmax in highly variable drugs.' This is INCORRECT. FDA allows RSABE primarily for Cmax only. AUC remains at standard 80-125% acceptance criteria regardless of variability, same as EMA. The actual divergence is that EMA applies RSABE to Cmax ONLY (correct in v2 file), while FDA also applies it to Cmax. Neither FDA nor EMA routinely applies RSABE to AUC.
critical_findings.source_evidence: EMA Bioequivalence Guideline Section 4.1.10 confirms AUC stays 80-125% regardless of variability. FDA RSABE guidance applies to Cmax.
critical_findings.action_required: Fix DIV-016 fda_requirement to state RSABE applies to Cmax, not both AUC and Cmax.

confirmed_examples: DIV-001: Multi-authority EU system vs single FDA — CONFIRMED via Reg 726/2004 Art 3-4 and 21 USC §355
DIV-006: 8+2+1 data exclusivity vs 5-year NCE — CONFIRMED via Dir 2001/83/EC Art 10(1)
DIV-010: EU CTR 536/2014 multi-state trial vs FDA IND — CONFIRMED
DIV-014: 25-year archival vs 2-year FDA — CONFIRMED via Reg 536/2014 Art 58 and 21 CFR 312.62(c)
DIV-019: QP system vs QCU — CONFIRMED via Dir 2001/83/EC Arts 48-52 and 21 CFR 211.22
DIV-042: DTC advertising prohibition vs FDA permission — CONFIRMED via Dir 2001/83/EC Art 88
DIV-045: ATMP 30-year traceability — CONFIRMED via Reg 1394/2007 Art 15
DIV-055: EU variation system vs FDA supplement system — CONFIRMED via Reg 1234/2008 and 21 CFR 314.70

needs_clarification.issue: QP qualification list incomplete — omits veterinary medicine and pharmaceutical chemistry/technology from eligible degrees per Art 49(2). Also omits that 2-year practical experience can be reduced to 1-1.5 years for longer university courses.
needs_clarification.fix: Add veterinary medicine and pharmaceutical chemistry/technology to QP degree list. Note experience reduction provisions.

needs_clarification.issue: Conditional MA description says 'prior to comprehensive clinical data' but EU conditional MA under Reg 726/2004 Art 14-a still requires positive benefit-risk — just on less complete data. The description is accurate but could be read as implying no clinical data needed.
needs_clarification.fix: Minor wording refinement to emphasize positive benefit-risk still required.

needs_clarification.issue: FDA retention claim says '2 years after approval of NDA/ANDA or investigation discontinuation.' The actual CFR text (312.62) is more specific: 2 years after the date a marketing application is approved for the drug for the indication being investigated, or 2 years after the investigation is discontinued and FDA is notified.
needs_clarification.fix: Tighten FDA retention language to match exact 312.62 wording.

needs_clarification.issue: SUSAR timelines described as 'similar with differences' but the timelines (7/15 days) are actually identical. Only the reporting destination and format differ.

Environmental risk assessment: claim says FDA 'most applications qualify for categorical exclusion.' While true, this characterization is regulatory practice, not statutory requirement. The regulation (21 CFR Part 25) does require EA; it is the categorical exclusion provisions that effectively exempt most drugs.
needs_clarification.fix: Minor — acknowledge that 21 CFR 25 technically requires EA but provides broad categorical exclusions.

confirmed_examples: DIV-001: QP batch certification (EU) vs QCU (FDA) — CONFIRMED. No personal statutory liability in FDA system.
DIV-002: Four-grade A/B/C/D cleanroom vs ISO classification — CONFIRMED via Annex 1 Tables 1 & 5
DIV-003: Mandatory CCS document — CONFIRMED via Annex 1 Section 2.3-2.6. No FDA equivalent.
DIV-004: PUPSIT mandatory — CONFIRMED via Annex 1 Section 8.87
DIV-006: Sinks/drains prohibited Grade A/B — CONFIRMED verbatim via Annex 1 Section 4.9
DIV-007: 100% CCIT for fusion-sealed <=100mL — CONFIRMED via Annex 1 Section 8.22
DIV-010: Site Master File EU-only — CONFIRMED
DIV-013: HBEL-based cleaning validation (EU) vs 1/1000th dose (FDA) — CONFIRMED
DIV-014: Per-container identity testing default (EU) vs per-lot (FDA) — CONFIRMED via Chapter 5/Annex 8 and 21 CFR 211.84
DIV-029: UV irradiation NOT acceptable sterilisation — CONFIRMED verbatim via Annex 1 Section 8.71

false_divergences.claimed_divergence: EMA RSABE widening for Cmax ONLY; FDA RSABE for BOTH AUC and Cmax. BCS biowaiver: EU Class I only; FDA Class I AND Class III.
false_divergences.reason: TWO ERRORS: (1) BCS biowaivers — post-ICH M9 (2019), both FDA and EMA accept Class I AND Class III. The claim has the positions reversed vs the v2 file and both are wrong. (2) RSABE scope — FDA does NOT allow RSABE for both AUC and Cmax. FDA applies RSABE to Cmax only, same as EMA. AUC remains 80-125% in both jurisdictions.
false_divergences.source: ICH M9 (2019) Section 2-3; EMA Bioequivalence Guideline Section 4.1.10; FDA RSABE guidance

needs_clarification.issue: Batch record retention: FDA claim says '1 year after batch expiry or 1 year after distribution, whichever is longer.' Actual 21 CFR 211.180(a) says '1 year after expiration date.' The 3-year-after-distribution alternative applies ONLY to certain OTC products lacking expiration dates. Not a general two-option rule.
needs_clarification.fix: Clarify that FDA retention is 1 year after expiry for standard products; 3-year alternative is OTC-specific.

needs_clarification.issue: Annual PQR: EU GMP Chapter 1.10 says 'regular periodic or rolling quality reviews' — not strictly 'annual.' Also lists ~13-14 elements, not exactly 12 as claimed.
needs_clarification.fix: Change 'annual' to 'regular periodic or rolling' and adjust element count.

needs_clarification.issue: Per-container identity testing: Annex 8 Section 2 allows reduced testing of containers 'where a validated procedure has been established.' The divergence file correctly identifies the default but should note the validated-procedure exception.
needs_clarification.fix: Add note that validated reduced-sampling procedure is permitted under Annex 8.

needs_clarification.issue: Medical examination: accurately states EU requirement. FDA side accurately states no mandatory exam. However, the claim uses Chapter 2.16 as reference — actual chapter numbering may be 2.15 depending on edition. Content is correct.
needs_clarification.fix: Verify exact section number against current GMP edition.

confirmed_examples: JP-002: ICH E5 bridging study requirement — CONFIRMED via PMDA_Ethnic_Factors_Bridging.pdf (ICH E5(R1))
JP-004: Ethnic sensitivity assessment framework — CONFIRMED via ICH E5(R1)
JP-006: JP18 pharmacopoeia compliance with unique monographs — CONFIRMED. 200+ crude drug monographs confirmed.
JP-008: JP18 biologics characterization G3 section — CONFIRMED. G3-1 through G3-15 subsections verified.
JP-005-ref: JP18 Section 6.17 protein drug particulate test — CONFIRMED as JP-unique test method.
JP-008: Japan stability Zone II 25C/60% RH — CONFIRMED via PMDA_Stability_Testing.pdf and ICH Q1A(R2)
JP-012: Japanese Package Insert unique format — CONFIRMED as fundamentally different from PI and SmPC
JP-013: Stability conditions harmonized with ICH Zone II — CONFIRMED
JP-015: Carcinogenicity aligned with ICH S1 — CONFIRMED via ICH S1A/S1B
JP-016: Crude drug/Kampo standards HIGH for herbal — CONFIRMED. JP18 pp 1861-2086.
JP-017: PBPK model validation requirements — CONFIRMED as more prescriptive than FDA/EMA

needs_clarification.issue: Re-examination system: 10-year orphan CONFIRMED from PMDA source. 8-year NCE period is well-established in Japanese pharmaceutical law but the specific statutory citation is not in the available source documents.
needs_clarification.fix: Add specific PAL/PMD Act citation for 8-year NCE re-examination period.

needs_clarification.issue: CYP2C19 polymorphism: 18-22% poor metabolizer frequency in Japanese vs 2-5% Caucasians. These statistics are widely accepted in pharmacogenomics literature but are not cited in the PMDA source documents. Should include a literature citation.
needs_clarification.fix: Add primary literature reference for CYP2C19 allele frequency data.

needs_clarification.issue: Transporter panel: Claim that PMDA requires OCT1 and FDA does not. The actual difference is relatively minor and evolving as FDA guidance updates. Current characterization is substantially correct but framing as a clear-cut divergence may overstate the practical impact.
needs_clarification.fix: Note that transporter evaluation is converging across jurisdictions; flag OCT1 as 'recommended' vs 'routinely required' rather than as a clear divergence.

cannot_verify.issue: Foreign Manufacturer Accreditation (PAL Article 13-3): The PAL/PMD Act text is not included in the source document collection. Claim is consistent with well-established PMDA practice but cannot be verified against source PDFs.
cannot_verify.note: Recommend adding PAL/PMD Act English translation to stage-1/pmda-guidelines/ for future verification.

cannot_verify.issue: Sorivudine/5-FU interaction precedent: Claim plausible and historically accurate (1993 incident is well-documented) but specific PMDA DDI guideline text referencing it is not in source documents.
cannot_verify.note: Low risk — this is historical fact, not a regulatory position that could be mischaracterized.

confirmed_examples: DIV-CA-004: Product Monograph 3-part format vs PI 17-section vs SmPC 10-section — CONFIRMED as completely different formats
DIV-CA-005: Establishment Licence with annual review — CONFIRMED via FDR cross-reference in A.01.047
DIV-CA-008: Bilingual English/French labelling — CONFIRMED via FDR A.01.015
DIV-CA-012: PM(NOC) patent linkage vs no EU patent linkage — CONFIRMED as fundamental divergence
DIV-CA-013: Fewer expedited pathways than FDA — CONFIRMED (no Breakthrough Therapy or Fast Track equivalent)
DIV-CA-014: DIN system vs NDC — CONFIRMED (HC-assigned vs manufacturer-assigned)
DIV-CA-015: CS-BE as Canada-unique document — CONFIRMED. No FDA or EMA equivalent.
DIV-CA-009: Environmental Assessment Statement required vs FDA categorical exclusions — CONFIRMED
DIV-CA-010: Schedule D biologics broad lot release — CONFIRMED

needs_clarification.issue: 300-day NDS review target: This is a Health Canada performance standard published in guidance, NOT codified in the Food and Drug Regulations themselves. C.08.002 defines NDS requirements but does not specify 300 days.
needs_clarification.fix: Clarify that 300-day target is an HC service standard, not a regulatory requirement.

needs_clarification.issue: 8 years data protection: The summary_statistics section at end of file says '6-year filing block + 8-year NOC block + 6-month pediatric extension (CUSMA-aligned)' which conflicts with the divergence body text saying simply '8 years data protection.' The CUSMA-aligned description appears more current.
needs_clarification.fix: Reconcile the two descriptions of Canada's data protection period within the same file.

needs_clarification.issue: Bilingual requirement: FDR A.01.015(1) actually says 'in either the French or the English language in addition to any other language' — meaning one official language is required for prescription drugs. Full bilingual (both English AND French) is required for OTC self-selection drugs per A.01.015(2). For Product Monograph specifically, bilingual IS required per HC guidance, but the statutory basis is more nuanced than stated.
needs_clarification.fix: Add nuance: FDR A.01.015 requires one official language for Rx labels; bilingual PM requirement comes from HC guidance/template requirements, not solely from A.01.015.

cannot_verify.issue: ANDS/CRP system (C.08.002.1): Framework consistent with FDR structure but specific section text not visible in the pages read of the large PDF.
CTA requirements (C.05.001-C.05.016): Process confirmed via HC guidance pages but exact regulatory section numbers not verified in FDR PDF.
ADR reporting (C.01.017-C.01.019): Section numbers plausible within FDR structure but not directly verified in pages read.

confirmed_examples: DIV-001: Standard BE acceptance criteria 80-125% harmonized — CONFIRMED
DIV-002: NTI drugs 90-111% interval (FDA/EMA aligned, Japan lacks explicit guidance) — CONFIRMED
DIV-003: HVDP/RSABE Cmax widening divergence — CONFIRMED via EMA Bioequivalence Guideline Section 4.1.10
DIV-005: Jurisdiction-specific reference products — CONFIRMED
DIV-006: Biosimilar pathway divergence (FDA interchangeability unique) — CONFIRMED
DIV-009: Stability conditions Zone II 25C/60% RH — CONFIRMED via ICH Q1A(R2)
DIV-010: Three different foreign manufacturer oversight systems — CONFIRMED
DIV-012: Carcinogenicity ICH S1 harmonized — CONFIRMED via ICH S1A/S1B
DIV-013: Three different labeling formats — CONFIRMED
DIV-015: Japan ethnic bridging unique — CONFIRMED via ICH E5(R1)
DIV-016: Pediatric requirements divergence (EMA most prescriptive, Japan no mandate) — CONFIRMED
DIV-018: AE reporting timelines (Japan drug use results surveys unique) — CONFIRMED
DIV-020: Expedited pathways divergence (Sakigake unique to Japan) — CONFIRMED
DIV-021: Data exclusivity 12/10/8 years for biologics — CONFIRMED

false_divergences.claimed_divergence: FDA: Class I only; EMA: Class I AND Class III accepted; PMDA: Class I accepted, Class III case-by-case
false_divergences.reason: Post-ICH M9 (Nov 2019, FDA adopted May 2021), ALL three jurisdictions accept both BCS Class I and Class III biowaivers. The claim reflects pre-2019 positions and is outdated. Remaining divergence is only in excipient similarity stringency and dissolution speed criteria for Class III, not in which classes are eligible.
false_divergences.source: ICH M9 (2019) Sections 2-3: 'BCS-based biowaivers are applicable to drug products where the drug substance(s) exhibit high solubility and, either high permeability (BCS Class I) or low permeability (BCS Class III).'

corrected_divergence.fda: Class I and Class III per ICH M9. Class III requires very rapid dissolution (>=85% in <=15 min) and qualitatively same/quantitatively very similar excipients.
corrected_divergence.ema: Class I and Class III per ICH M9. EMA adopted even before M9 via 2010 Bioequivalence guideline Appendix III.
corrected_divergence.pmda: Class I and Class III per ICH M9. Some additional case-by-case consideration.
corrected_divergence.actual_divergence: Minor — differences in excipient similarity criteria and dissolution speed thresholds for Class III. Not a fundamental eligibility difference.

needs_clarification.issue: Orphan exclusivity: Claims 'Japan offers longest potential exclusivity (10 years)' but EU also offers 10 years. Both are tied for longest. Japan's 15% tax credit is described as 'highest' which is likely correct but represents the enhanced maximum rate, not the standard rate.
needs_clarification.fix: Change 'longest' to 'tied for longest with EU at 10 years.' Clarify tax credit as maximum enhanced rate.

needs_clarification.issue: Pregnancy risk categorization: Claims Japan maintains 'unique Japanese categories.' While Japan does have its own labeling approach, it has been evolving toward narrative-based descriptions similar to FDA/EMA. The characterization may be somewhat outdated.
needs_clarification.fix: Verify current PMDA pregnancy labeling format and update if Japan has shifted to narrative approach.

cross_file_consistency_issues.issue: BCS BIOWAIVER POSITIONS DIRECTLY CONTRADICT between v2 and v3 files
cross_file_consistency_issues.file_1_claim: FDA: Class I only; EMA: Class I AND Class III
cross_file_consistency_issues.file_2_claim: EU: Class I only; FDA: Class I AND Class III
cross_file_consistency_issues.resolution: BOTH ARE WRONG. Post-ICH M9, all three accept Class I and III. v2 has outdated pre-2019 positions; v3 has positions reversed AND outdated.
cross_file_consistency_issues.severity: CRITICAL — would give clients directly wrong advice about whether to conduct in vivo BE studies

cross_file_consistency_issues.file_2: output-v2/regulations-crl/jurisdiction-divergences.json DIV-017 says EU = 10 years, Japan = 10 years
cross_file_consistency_issues.resolution: CONSISTENT — both correctly state EU 10 years. Minor issue: v2 calls Japan 'longest' when tied with EU.

cross_file_consistency_issues.issue: Data exclusivity periods consistent across files
cross_file_consistency_issues.resolution: CONSISTENT across all files. Small-molecule and biologic exclusivity periods correctly differentiated.

source_gap_recommendations.gap: Japanese Pharmaceutical Affairs Law (PAL/PMD Act) English translation not in source collection
source_gap_recommendations.impact: Cannot verify JP-001 (Foreign Manufacturer Accreditation) and re-examination statutory basis
source_gap_recommendations.recommendation: Add official PAL/PMD Act English translation to stage-1/pmda-guidelines/

source_gap_recommendations.gap: PMDA DDI guideline (2019) not in source collection
source_gap_recommendations.impact: Cannot verify JP-009 (transporter panel), JP-011 (sorivudine precedent), JP-017 (PBPK requirements)
source_gap_recommendations.recommendation: Add PMDA Drug Interaction guideline (2019) to stage-1/pmda-guidelines/

source_gap_recommendations.gap: Health Canada Food and Drug Regulations PDF is very large (11.8MB) — deep sections (C.05, C.08) difficult to access
source_gap_recommendations.impact: Cannot verify specific section numbers for CTA, ANDS, data protection
source_gap_recommendations.recommendation: Extract relevant sections (C.01A, C.02, C.05, C.08) into separate searchable files

source_gap_recommendations.gap: PM(NOC) Regulations are a separate instrument not included in source collection
source_gap_recommendations.impact: Cannot verify patent linkage system details
